Apollo, Relay, urql, TanStack Query, persisted queries, and rolling your own. Here is what each one actually wins at, and the question I now ask before choosing.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

The dashboard that broke me loaded in 4.7 seconds.
It was a perfectly normal admin dashboard. Six widgets. Each widget asked for a few entities from our GraphQL API. The shape of the queries was clean. The database was fast. The server responded in under 100ms. And yet, on every page load, the user stared at six spinners for almost five seconds while the same user object was fetched from the network six separate times.
The bug was not the dashboard. The bug was that we had picked a caching strategy in 2023 without really understanding what we were choosing, and now we were paying the bill every single page load.
I spent the next two months rewriting the caching layer. In the process I shipped four different strategies and benchmarked two more. Here is the honest comparison, written for the version of me who would have saved an entire quarter if someone had handed it to him on day one.
